task: Author a Smithers workflow with two tasks that run in order inside a <Sequence>. The first is an agent task `extract` that reads a file and emits {url:string}. The second, `fetch`, is a deterministic compute task (NO agent, no LLM) whose children is a function that receives the first task's output and returns {status:number}. Wire the dependency so `fetch` reads `extract`'s output through a typed `deps` binding (map the dep key to the real task id with `needs`). Use `outputs` Zod schemas for both task outputs.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="pipe"><Sequence><Task id="extract" output={outputs.url} agent={reader}>Read the file.</Task><Task id="fetch" output={outputs.status} needs={{ url: "extract" }} deps={{ url: outputs.url }}>{(deps) => ({ status: probe(deps.url.url) })}</Task></Sequence></Workflow>
notes: Sequence + an agent Task then a compute Task that reads the prior output via needs (key->id map) and typed deps.

task: I have three independent lint/typecheck/test agent tasks that are expensive. Write a workflow that runs all three inside a <Parallel> but caps it so at most TWO execute at once, give each task a human-readable `label`, then add a final `report` task that only starts after all three finish (use an explicit dependency, not needs). Wrap the whole thing so report runs after the parallel group.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="checks"><Sequence><Parallel maxConcurrency={2}><Task id="lint" label="Lint" output={outputs.r} agent={a}>lint</Task><Task id="types" label="Typecheck" output={outputs.r} agent={a}>tsc</Task><Task id="test" label="Tests" output={outputs.r} agent={a}>test</Task></Parallel><Task id="report" output={outputs.report} dependsOn={["lint","types","test"]} agent={a}>summarize</Task></Sequence></Workflow>
notes: Parallel maxConcurrency={2}, per-task label, and a join Task gated with dependsOn (array of node ids).

task: Write a refine loop: a writer agent task drafts, a reviewer agent task approves or not, and the loop repeats until the latest review's `approved` is true. Cap it at 8 iterations, and when the cap is hit return the last result instead of failing the run. Because each pass adds to history, checkpoint the loop every 4 iterations to bound the durable state. Give the <Loop> a stable key.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="refine"><Loop key="refine" until={ctx.latest("review","review")?.approved === true} maxIterations={8} onMaxReached="return-last" continueAsNewEvery={4}><Task id="draft" output={outputs.draft} agent={writer}>draft</Task><Task id="review" output={outputs.review} agent={reviewer}>review</Task></Loop></Workflow>
notes: Loop with until (boolean condition), maxIterations, onMaxReached=return-last, continueAsNewEvery, and a key.

task: In a <Loop>, what are the two accepted values of the `onMaxReached` prop (what happens when `maxIterations` is hit), and which one is the default?
canonicalAnswer: fail, return-last
notes: onMaxReached is either 'fail' or 'return-last'; default is 'return-last'.

task: I want to repeat a block of tasks until a condition holds. I tried wrapping them in <While> and <Repeat> but Smithers says they don't exist. Which component actually does this, and what are its props for the stop condition and the iteration cap?
canonicalAnswer: Loop, until, maxIterations
notes: The component is <Loop>; `until` is the stop condition and `maxIterations` caps the passes. <While>/<Repeat>/<For> do not exist.

task: A prior `classify` task labels an incoming ticket as `bug` or `other`. Write a workflow that, after classify, routes with a <Branch>: if it's a bug, run a `triage` agent task; otherwise run a `route-to-human` task. The Branch must pick its subtree from the then/else props (Branch takes no children).
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="ticket"><Sequence><Task id="classify" output={outputs.cls} agent={a}>classify</Task><Branch if={ctx.latest("classify","cls")?.label === "bug"} then={<Task id="triage" output={outputs.t} agent={a}>triage</Task>} else={<Task id="route" output={outputs.t} agent={a}>route</Task>} /></Sequence></Workflow>
notes: Branch with if + then + else; else is the fallback subtree. No JSX children on Branch.

task: How do I conditionally run one of two subtrees in a Smithers workflow? I tried <If>/<Else> and a <Switch> and none of them render. Which component does conditional routing, and which props hold the two branches?
canonicalAnswer: Branch, then, else
notes: Use <Branch if={...} then={...} else={...} />. <If>/<Else>/<Switch> do not exist.

task: Author a deploy saga using the array-driven API: a <Saga> whose `steps` array has two steps. Step `create-pr` has an action task and a `close-pr` compensation task; step `deploy-staging` has an action and a `rollback-staging` compensation. If a later step fails, the already-completed steps' compensations must run in reverse. Set onFailure so it compensates (and does not additionally fail the run).
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="deploy"><Saga id="dep" onFailure="compensate" steps={[ { id: "create-pr", action: <Task id="create-pr" output={outputs.pr} agent={a}>open</Task>, compensation: <Task id="close-pr" output={outputs.x} agent={a}>close</Task> }, { id: "deploy-staging", action: <Task id="deploy-staging" output={outputs.s} agent={a}>deploy</Task>, compensation: <Task id="rollback-staging" output={outputs.x} agent={a}>rollback</Task> } ]} /></Workflow>
notes: Saga with the steps[] array; each entry has action + compensation; onFailure='compensate'.

task: Same two-step deploy saga (create-pr with a close-pr compensation, deploy-staging with a rollback-staging compensation), but write it using the JSX children form with <Saga.Step> elements instead of the `steps` array. Each <Saga.Step> needs a `compensation` prop and a single action child.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="deploy"><Saga id="dep"><Saga.Step id="create-pr" compensation={<Task id="close-pr" output={outputs.x} agent={a}>close</Task>}><Task id="create-pr" output={outputs.pr} agent={a}>open</Task></Saga.Step><Saga.Step id="deploy-staging" compensation={<Task id="rollback-staging" output={outputs.x} agent={a}>rollback</Task>}><Task id="deploy-staging" output={outputs.s} agent={a}>deploy</Task></Saga.Step></Saga></Workflow>
notes: Saga with <Saga.Step> JSX children, each carrying a compensation prop and one action child.

task: A <Saga>'s `onFailure` prop controls what happens when a step fails. What are its three accepted values, and which is the default? I specifically want compensations to run AND the run to still end as failed afterward — which value is that?
canonicalAnswer: compensate, compensate-and-fail, fail
notes: onFailure is one of compensate | compensate-and-fail | fail; default is 'compensate'. 'compensate-and-fail' runs compensations then fails the run.

task: After kicking off a deploy, write a <Poller> that checks readiness by calling a plain compute function (NOT an agent) that returns an object containing `satisfied: boolean`. Poll up to 20 times with exponential backoff starting at a 3000ms base interval, and when it never becomes ready, return the last check result instead of failing. Store the check result via a checkOutput schema.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="ready"><Poller id="ready" backoff="exponential" intervalMs={3000} maxAttempts={20} onTimeout="return-last" check={async () => ({ satisfied: await isUp() })} checkOutput={outputs.ready}>Is the deploy healthy?</Poller></Workflow>
notes: Poller with backoff='exponential', intervalMs, maxAttempts, onTimeout='return-last', a compute check fn, and checkOutput including satisfied:boolean.

task: For the <Poller> component, list the accepted `backoff` strategies, and tell me the default behavior of `onTimeout` when maxAttempts is exhausted.
canonicalAnswer: fixed, linear, exponential, fail
notes: backoff is one of fixed | linear | exponential (default fixed). onTimeout defaults to 'fail'.

task: Build a workflow that first waits 30 minutes with a <Timer> (relative duration), then durably suspends on a <WaitForEvent> until an external `payment.captured` event arrives. Match the right event instance with a correlationId set to the order id, validate the payload with an output schema, time out after 2 hours, and on timeout continue with null rather than failing. Mark the wait `async` so unrelated branches aren't blocked.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="order"><Sequence><Timer id="cooldown" duration="30m" /><WaitForEvent id="paid" event="payment.captured" correlationId={ctx.input.orderId} output={outputs.payment} outputSchema={paymentSchema} timeoutMs={7200000} onTimeout="continue" async /></Sequence></Workflow>
notes: Timer duration='30m' then WaitForEvent with event, correlationId, output+outputSchema, timeoutMs, onTimeout='continue', async (durable deferred resolution).

task: On the <Timer> component, which prop fires at an absolute ISO timestamp / Date, and which prop waits a relative duration string like "30m"?
canonicalAnswer: until, duration
notes: `until` takes an absolute ISO timestamp or Date; `duration` takes a relative string ('500ms','1s','30m','1h','7d').

task: Author an isolated build workflow: a <Worktree> at an ABSOLUTE path on a feature branch, based off `main`. Inside it run a build and a test agent task in <Parallel>, then funnel the apply/merge step through a <MergeQueue> so at most one merge happens at a time. Do not pin a cwd on the agents.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="iso"><Worktree path="/tmp/smithers/wt-feat" branch="feat/x" baseBranch="main"><Parallel maxConcurrency={2}><Task id="build" output={outputs.b} agent={a}>build</Task><Task id="test" output={outputs.t} agent={a}>test</Task></Parallel><MergeQueue maxConcurrency={1}><Task id="apply" output={outputs.m} agent={a}>merge</Task></MergeQueue></Worktree></Workflow>
notes: Worktree with path + branch selection + baseBranch fallback to main, Parallel build/test, MergeQueue (maxConcurrency=1) for the merge.

task: I gave my <Worktree> a relative `path` of "wt-a" and expected it to land next to my workflow .tsx file, but it showed up somewhere else. Separately, I set `baseBranch` to the output of `jj log -r @`. Explain where a relative worktree path actually resolves, how to make the location deterministic, and why passing the current `@` change as baseBranch is wrong.
notes: Must say a relative path resolves against the LAUNCH ROOT (the --root, else nearest ancestor with a .smithers/ package, else the operator cwd), NEVER dirname(workflowFile); recommend an absolute path (e.g. /tmp/smithers/wt-a) and reading it back with ctx.worktreePath/ctx.resolveWorktreePath. Must say baseBranch should be a stable committed branch (defaults to 'main'); passing jj `@` snapshots uncommitted changes into an undescribed commit that jj refuses to push. Must NOT claim relative paths resolve relative to the workflow file's directory.

task: I have an existing `reviewWorkflow` exported from another module. Author a parent workflow that invokes it as a <Subflow> which gets its OWN run row (not embedded inline), passing it `{ pr: 42 }` as input and storing its result. Reference the child via the `workflow` prop.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="parent"><Subflow id="review" workflow={reviewWorkflow} mode="childRun" input={{ pr: 42 }} output={outputs.review} /></Workflow>
notes: Subflow with workflow prop, mode='childRun' (own run row), input passing, and output target.

task: The <Subflow> component has a `mode` prop. What are its two values, and which one embeds the child in the parent run versus giving it its own run row?
canonicalAnswer: childRun, inline
notes: mode is 'childRun' (own DB row/run) or 'inline' (embeds in parent run).

task: Build the context-handoff pattern: a <Loop> does incremental work, wrapped in a <TryCatchFinally> that catches ONLY the `ASPECT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` error code. When that code fires, the catch branch renders a <ContinueAsNew> carrying just the distilled state into a fresh run. Any other error code should propagate (not be caught).
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="handoff"><TryCatchFinally catchErrors={["ASPECT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED"]} catch={<ContinueAsNew state={distilled} />} try={<Loop id="work" until={done} maxIterations={50}><Task id="step" output={outputs.step} agent={worker}>work</Task></Loop>} /></Workflow>
notes: TryCatchFinally with catchErrors=['ASPECT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED'], try=<Loop>, catch=<ContinueAsNew state={...}> carrying distilled state.

task: Author a workflow that durably waits for an external `approve-deploy` signal carrying { approver:string, ok:boolean } (validated by a Zod schema), correlated by the release id. Mark it `async` so other branches keep running. Use the <Signal> children-callback form so that once the signal arrives, you render a follow-up task that only runs when `ok` is true.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="rel"><Signal id="gate" schema={approveSchema} correlationId={ctx.input.releaseId} async>{(data) => data.ok ? <Task id="ship" output={outputs.ship} agent={a}>ship</Task> : null}</Signal></Workflow>
notes: Signal with schema, correlationId, async, and a children callback (data)=>ReactNode rendering a follow-up Task.

task: Write a single flaky deploy <Task> hardened for production: retry up to 4 times with an exponential retryPolicy, a 10-minute timeoutMs, a 90s heartbeatTimeoutMs to catch a hung agent, a fallbackAgent to try if the primary keeps failing, continueOnFail so the run doesn't die if it ultimately fails, a cache policy, and a friendly label. Put a downstream `notify` task after it in a <Sequence>.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="deploy"><Sequence><Task id="deploy" label="Deploy to prod" output={outputs.dep} agent={primary} fallbackAgent={backup} retries={4} retryPolicy={{ backoff: "exponential", initialDelayMs: 1000 }} timeoutMs={600000} heartbeatTimeoutMs={90000} continueOnFail cache={{ scope: "run" }}>deploy</Task><Task id="notify" output={outputs.n} agent={a}>notify</Task></Sequence></Workflow>
notes: One Task with retries, retryPolicy (exponential), timeoutMs, heartbeatTimeoutMs, fallbackAgent, continueOnFail, cache, label; followed by a downstream task in a Sequence.

task: Which single boolean prop on a <Task> disables retries entirely (equivalent to writing retries={0})?
canonicalAnswer: noRetry
notes: The prop is `noRetry` (boolean). It is equivalent to retries={0}.

task: I have a risky `migrate-db` agent task that needs human approval before it runs, but I do NOT want unrelated work (a `build-docs` task) to be blocked while approval is pending. Author a workflow where migrate-db is gated with needsApproval but waits asynchronously, restrict the agent to a safe tool allowlist, and skip it entirely when ctx.input.dryRun is true. Run build-docs alongside it via <Parallel>.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="release"><Parallel><Task id="migrate-db" output={outputs.m} agent={a} needsApproval async allowTools={["Bash","Read"]} skipIf={ctx.input.dryRun === true}>migrate</Task><Task id="build-docs" output={outputs.d} agent={a}>docs</Task></Parallel></Workflow>
notes: Task with needsApproval + async (non-blocking approval wait) + allowTools allowlist + skipIf, running in Parallel with an unrelated task.

task: Add cross-run memory to a single agent <Task> so that before it runs it recalls relevant facts from a namespace and injects them into the prompt context, and after it completes it writes a fact back to that namespace for future runs. Show the `memory` prop configuration on the Task.
notes: Correct answer puts a `memory` config object on the <Task> (the memory prop / TaskMemoryConfig): it should specify recall (e.g. a namespace and topK to pull relevant facts before running) and a write/remember step after completion. Must use the Task `memory` prop rather than inventing a separate <Memory> component.

task: Attach automatic scoring to an agent <Task> that answers a question: run scorers on its output after completion, supply the expected reference answer that comparison scorers grade against, and supply additional source context for a faithfulness scorer. Also override the agent output shape with an explicit outputSchema. Show the props.
notes: Correct answer uses the Task `scorers` prop (a ScorersMap), the `groundTruth` prop for the expected/reference answer fed to comparison scorers, the `context` prop for extra source context used by scorers such as faithfulnessScorer, and an `outputSchema` ZodObject overriding/declaring the agent output shape. Must not confuse groundTruth with context.

task: I want a `summarize` agent task to start from a COPY of the conversation/session another agent task `explore` ended with, without mutating `explore`. How do I express that on the <Task>, and what are the rules — does it create a dependency, what kind of task can be a fork source, and how does it behave inside a <Loop>?
notes: Correct answer uses the Task `fork` prop set to the source task id. It must say: the fork source becomes an implicit dependency (this task waits for the source to complete), it copies the source's final agent session snapshot into a fresh independent session and submits its own prompt, the source is never mutated, the fork source must be an AGENT task (fork requires an agent task), and inside a <Loop> it resolves to the latest completed snapshot for that id.

task: What does wrapping tasks in a <Sequence> guarantee versus just listing them, and when would I set the `key` prop on a <Sequence>?
notes: Correct answer: <Sequence> runs its children strictly in order (each child only after the previous completes), as opposed to the default sibling scheduling / <Parallel>. The `key` prop gives the node a stable reconciler identity (useful when a Sequence is generated in a list/loop or rendered conditionally) so its state/outputs map to the same node across re-renders.

task: Which boolean prop on the top-level <Workflow> component turns the workflow-level result cache on or off?
canonicalAnswer: cache
notes: The <Workflow> `cache` boolean prop toggles workflow-level result caching.

task: A <Task> can produce its output three ways depending on its `agent` prop and what its children is. Describe the three modes: static, compute, and agent — what children/props each uses and when the LLM is (or isn't) called.
notes: Correct answer distinguishes: STATIC = no agent, children is a literal value/object (e.g. {{ value: 1 }}) used directly as the output row — no LLM. COMPUTE = no agent, children is a function (sync or async) that returns the output row — runs real code, no LLM. AGENT = an `agent` prop is set and children is the string prompt — the agent/LLM is invoked and its (schema-validated) output becomes the row.

task: Author a production release pipeline as ONE Smithers workflow. (1) Do all work inside an isolated <Worktree> on a release branch based off main. (2) Run build + unit-test agent tasks in <Parallel>. (3) Then a <Saga> performs the risky deploy steps (publish artifact -> migrate db -> flip traffic), each step with a compensation that undoes it, compensating in reverse on failure. (4) Before the traffic flip, require human approval on that task. (5) After deploy, a <Loop> reruns smoke tests until they pass, capped at 5 iterations. Compose it all correctly.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="release"><Worktree path="/tmp/smithers/rel" branch="release/v2" baseBranch="main"><Parallel maxConcurrency={2}><Task id="build" output={outputs.b} agent={a}>build</Task><Task id="unit" output={outputs.u} agent={a}>test</Task></Parallel><Saga id="deploy" onFailure="compensate" steps={[ { id: "publish", action: <Task id="publish" output={outputs.p} agent={a}>publish</Task>, compensation: <Task id="unpublish" output={outputs.x} agent={a}>unpublish</Task> }, { id: "migrate", action: <Task id="migrate" output={outputs.m} agent={a}>migrate</Task>, compensation: <Task id="rollback" output={outputs.x} agent={a}>rollback</Task> }, { id: "flip", action: <Task id="flip" output={outputs.f} agent={a} needsApproval>flip traffic</Task>, compensation: <Task id="unflip" output={outputs.x} agent={a}>revert traffic</Task> } ]} /><Loop id="smoke" until={ctx.latest("smoke","s")?.passed === true} maxIterations={5}><Task id="smoke" output={outputs.s} agent={a}>smoke test</Task></Loop></Worktree></Workflow>
notes: Complex: Worktree (baseBranch main) > Parallel build/test > Saga with per-step compensation + needsApproval on the flip step > Loop smoke-until-pass. Tags: Worktree, Parallel, Saga, Loop, Task.

task: Author an async, event-driven release-gate workflow. (1) Durably suspend on a <WaitForEvent> for an external `ci.passed` webhook, correlated by commit sha, with a 6-hour timeoutMs and onTimeout that fails the run; mark it async. (2) In parallel, allow a human to short-circuit via a <Signal> ('override', correlated by sha, with a Zod schema and children callback). (3) Add a <Timer> that fires after 1 hour to escalate. (4) Once gated, a <Poller> (exponential backoff) waits for the deploy to report healthy. Wrap the whole thing in a <TryCatchFinally> whose finally always posts a status comment.
canonicalAnswer: <Workflow name="gate"><TryCatchFinally finally={<Task id="status" output={outputs.st} agent={a}>post status</Task>} try={<Sequence><Parallel><WaitForEvent id="ci" event="ci.passed" correlationId={ctx.input.sha} output={outputs.ci} timeoutMs={21600000} onTimeout="fail" async /><Signal id="override" schema={ovSchema} correlationId={ctx.input.sha} async>{(d) => d.force ? <Task id="forced" output={outputs.f} agent={a}>force</Task> : null}</Signal><Timer id="escalate" duration="1h" /></Parallel><Poller id="health" backoff="exponential" intervalMs={5000} maxAttempts={30} check={async () => ({ satisfied: await healthy() })} checkOutput={outputs.health}>healthy?</Poller></Sequence>} /></Workflow>
notes: Complex async: TryCatchFinally(finally) wrapping Parallel(WaitForEvent async + Signal async callback + Timer) then a Poller(exponential). Tags: TryCatchFinally, WaitForEvent, Signal, Timer, Poller.
